full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Clarifying the cutting-plane step in Algorithm 1
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: In the FGM paper Section 2.2, Algorithm 1 says we add the 'most violated d_hat' to the working set. What does 'most violated' mean in terms of the constraint — is it the d that maximises the LHS of the constraint in Eq. 4?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: It confirmed that we are looking for the constraint violation. I re-read Section 2.4 myself and saw that sorting by c_j^2 gives the exact solution, so I used that to write the step-by-step description in my own words.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Checked Eq. 4 and Section 2.4 — the paper does say the most violated d is obtained by taking top B features by c_j^2. I wrote the algorithm steps in the notebook without copying.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Summarising the paper's claim in one sentence
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: How would you state in one sentence what problem the FGM paper solves and what makes it better than standard SVM-RFE? I want to phrase it for my own summary, not copy.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: It gave a template like 'solves X, better because Y'. I rephrased it completely after re-reading the abstract and Section 1 — the paper says they get a sparse solution with a single MKL-style formulation instead of repeated RFE.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: My final sentence in the notebook matches the abstract and Section 1. I did not paste the LLM's sentence.
